text.title: Monorepo with Turborepo Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed when applying monorepo-turborepo. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix keyed to the methodology.

antipattern.symptom: Repo has package-lock.json + pnpm-lock.yaml; developers install with whatever they have; transitive deps differ.
antipattern.root-cause: pnpm migration started but not finished.
antipattern.fix: Delete non-pnpm lockfiles. Add CI check that fails on `package-lock.json` or `yarn.lock` presence. Verify pnpm version pinned via packageManager field.

antipattern.symptom: Build succeeds on dev machine, fails in CI because a transitive dep released a breaking patch.
antipattern.root-cause: Versions use `^` ranges; minor differences in installed deps.
antipattern.fix: Pin all direct deps exactly. Set up Renovate to propose upgrades as PRs.

antipattern.symptom: turbo build re-runs everything every time; CI takes 20 minutes per push.
antipattern.root-cause: Tasks declared without outputs; turbo cannot cache.
antipattern.fix: Add outputs to every task. Verify cache hit rate > 70% after the next CI run; iterate if not.

antipattern.symptom: Root scripts include `prebuild: pnpm -r run prebuild` that bypasses turbo; cache miss across the board.
antipattern.root-cause: Author wanted finer control; bypassed pipeline.
antipattern.fix: Migrate to a turbo task with proper dependsOn. Remove direct -r commands from root scripts.
